differential_diagnoses: Portal biliopathy with associated portal hypertensive gastropathy/duodenopathy
Hemobilia secondary to pseudoaneurysm from prior hepaticojejunostomy
Chronic portal vein thrombosis with cavernous transformation
Hepatoportal sclerosis or non-cirrhotic portal hypertension
Dieulafoy's lesion in the remnant biliary/enteric anatomy
next_steps: Digital subtraction angiography (selective celiac and mesenteric) to identify arterial pseudoaneurysm or arteriovenous fistula
Interventional radiology consultation for portal venous system assessment and management of portal hypertension
Surgical consultation regarding the prior hepaticojejunostomy site and potential biliary source of bleeding
Repeat EGD focusing on careful evaluation of the hepaticojejunostomy site for stomal varices
Liver biopsy to assess for underlying portal hypertensive changes or infiltrative disease
Serum tumor markers (CA 19-9, CEA) to rule out occult malignancy
